name: ASAP Credit Repair & Financial Education
description_short: Family-owned credit repair company founded in 2008 that disputes derogatory items on credit reports using a flat-fee model rather than monthly subscriptions.
description_long:
ASAP Credit Repair is a family-owned and operated credit repair company founded in 2008 by Joe Mahlow, who spent 17+ years in the credit, auto, and mortgage industries before launching the business. The company has grown from serving clients in a single state to operating nationwide, claiming over 1,500 Google reviews and 20+ years of combined industry experience. According to their website, the founder created ASAP after recognizing that over half of his lending clients were unable to purchase homes or vehicles due to poor credit.

The company's core service is disputing derogatory items and errors on credit reports across the three major credit bureaus (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion). They offer a free credit assessment completed in under 2 minutes, free attorney advice, and claim personalized credit consultations. Their pricing model charges a flat one-time fee of approximately $499 rather than recurring monthly fees, and they market an average success rate of 73% with results expected in 60-90 days.

ASAP positions itself as distinct from competitors by disputing all derogatory items simultaneously rather than one item per month, assigning each client a dedicated senior consultant and account manager, and emphasizing employee happiness as a business priority. They highlight that the average credit repair business lasts only 7 months, implying their longevity since 2008 demonstrates different business practices. Their website includes blog content on specific strategies like the "CEO Method" for removing late payments and financial education topics such as building credit for children.

The company operates transparently about their model but lacks third-party verification of claimed success rates and timelines. No independent consumer protection records, ratings from the FTC, BBB, or state attorney general offices are provided on their website. The claim of 73% average success rate and 60-90 day timeline are self-reported metrics without external validation. Consumers should verify credentials and understand that credit repair results depend heavily on the validity of disputed items and individual credit file accuracy. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Credit Repair Program
features: Credit report analysis across all three bureaus
Dispute filing for inaccurate and unverifiable items

pros: Flat one-time fee of ~$499 instead of recurring monthly charges, reducing long-term costs
Disputes all derogatory items simultaneously rather than one per month, potentially faster results
Assigns dedicated senior consultant and account manager to each client for personalized service
Offers free credit assessment, attorney advice, and no-contract consultations
Company founded in 2008 with 17+ years of founder experience in credit and lending industries
Operates nationwide, helping clients across all U.S. states
Provides financial education content including blog posts on late payment removal and credit building
cons: Claimed 73% success rate and 60-90 day timeline are self-reported without independent third-party verification
No mention of BBB accreditation, FTC registration, or state licensing verification on website
Marketing language uses comparative claims ('no comparison') without substantiating competitor data
Website does not disclose any regulatory compliance, dispute process timelines, or what constitutes 'success'
No transparent information about refund policy, cancellation terms, or guarantee details despite 'cancel anytime' claim
best_for: Consumers with multiple derogatory items (late payments, collections, charge-offs) seeking simultaneous dispute
Individuals who prefer one flat fee over monthly subscription credit repair models
People wanting personalized account management and dedicated consultant relationships
Borrowers preparing to apply for major loans (home, auto) and needing credit improvement before application
diagnosis: ASAP Credit Repair is best suited for consumers with multiple negative items on their credit report who prefer flat-fee pricing and want simultaneous dispute action rather than monthly item-by-item challenges. The main caveat is that claimed success rates and timelines are unverified; consumers should request references, understand the FTC's Credit Repair Organizations Act restrictions, and verify that disputed items are actually inaccurate before engaging, as legitimate negative information cannot be legally removed. Consumers comparing credit repair companies should also consider whether credit monitoring services, secured credit cards, or credit counseling might address their needs alongside or instead of paid credit repair services.
services: Credit report dispute filing with three major bureaus (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)
Free credit assessment completed in under 2 minutes
Personalized credit consultation with no contracts
Dedicated senior consultant and account manager assignment
Derogatory item removal (late payments, collections, charge-offs)
Late payment removal using 'CEO Method' strategy
Credit score improvement planning and tracking
Financial education and credit building guidance
Child credit building assistance and education
Nationwide service across all U.S. states
Post-repair credit maintenance and monitoring recommendations
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
